trigger_condition: component re-renders while success toast is visible (e.g., Rule edit triggers playbook state change)
guard_snippet: useEffect(() => { const timer = setTimeout(dismiss, 5000); return () => clearTimeout(timer); }, [type, dismiss])
potential_consequence: Timer resets on every re-render. Success toast stays visible longer than 5 seconds.

trigger_condition: showError called twice in quick succession (e.g., rapid double-click on export)
guard_snippet: setFeedback({ type: 'error', message, retry }) — replaces previous feedback
potential_consequence: Second call overwrites first. If first error had a different message, user sees only the last one.

trigger_condition: retry callback is stale (captures old closure from render cycle)
guard_snippet: retry is stored in useState at showError time
potential_consequence: If state changes between error show and retry click, retry handler operates on outdated state.

trigger_condition: message string is very long (e.g., 500+ characters)
potential_consequence: Long message overflows toast width. No word-break or truncation applied.

trigger_condition: handleDownloadGeneratedFiles is called but component has unmounted before async completion
potential_consequence: React state update on unmounted component warning. Toast state is set after navigation/unmount.

trigger_condition: dismiss is called while setTimeout is pending
guard_snippet: useEffect cleanup clears timer on unmount, but dismiss doesn't clear it
potential_consequence: If user manually dismisses a success toast, the timer still fires dismiss again (no-op since state already null). Harmless but unnecessary setTimeout execution.

trigger_condition: feedback.type is 'success' and feedback.retry is non-null (theoretically impossible but type allows it)
potential_consequence: Handled correctly: retry button only appears for error type.
